title: Check Isomorphic Strings

content.problemStatement:
You are given two strings, sss and ttt. Determine whether these two strings are `isomorphic`.

Two strings are said to be **isomorphic** if characters in string sss can be replaced to get string ttt, such that:


Each character in sss maps to exactly one character in ttt.
No two different characters in sss map to the same character in ttt.
The order of characters is preserved.
A character is allowed to map to itself.
Function DeclarationFunction NameisIsomorphicisIsomorphicisIsomorphic – This function checks whether two strings are isomorphic.

Parameters
sss : A string representing the source string.
ttt : A string representing the target string.
Return Value
Returns truetruetrue if the strings sss and ttt are isomorphic.
Returns falsefalsefalse otherwise.
Constraints
1≤N≤1001 \leq N \leq 1001≤N≤100
1≤∣s∣=∣t∣≤1051 \leq |s| = |t| \leq 10^51≤∣s∣=∣t∣≤105
Strings sss and ttt consist of ASCII characters.

Input Format

The first line contains a single integer NNN — the number of test cases.
For each test case:

The first line contains the string sss.
The second line contains the string ttt.




Output Format

For each test case, print:

`"YES"` if the strings are isomorphic.
`"NO"` otherwise.
content.inputFormat: First line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. For each test case, the first line contains the string s and the second line contains the string t. Both strings consist of ASCII characters and have equal length.
content.outputFormat: For each test case print "YES" if s and t are isomorphic, otherwise print "NO". Each answer on its own line.

sampleTestCases.explanation: 'mno' can be mapped to 'pqr' with a one‑to‑one correspondence, so YES. In 'hello' vs 'world' the character 'l' would need to map to both 'r' and 'l', violating the bijection, so NO.

sampleTestCases.input:
3
abcabc
xyzxyz
pqrpqr
mnopmn
abcd
abcc
sampleTestCases.explanation: First pair maps a→x,b→y,c→z consistently. Second pair fails because 'p' maps to both 'm' and 'n'. Third pair fails because the last 'd' would need to map to two different characters.
e→a and g→d give a valid bijection, so the strings are isomorphic.

solution.approach: Two strings are isomorphic iff there exists a bijection between the characters of s and t that respects the order. We maintain two arrays (or hash maps) of size 256 – one mapping characters from s to t and another from t to s. While scanning both strings simultaneously, we ensure that a previously seen character always maps to the same counterpart and that no two different characters map to the same one. If any conflict appears the strings are not isomorphic. This runs in linear time.
solution.timeComplexity: O(L) per test case, where L is the length of the strings (total O(sum of lengths) over all test cases).
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) – only two fixed-size arrays of length 256 are used.

code.Java: import java.io.*;public class Main{private static boolean isIsomorphic(String s,String t){if(s.length()!=t.length())return false;int[] mapST=new int[256];int[] mapTS=new int[256];for(int i=0;i<s.length();i++){int c1=s.charAt(i)&0xff;int c2=t.charAt(i)&0xff;if(mapST[c1]==0)mapST[c1]=c2+1;else if(mapST[c1]!=c2+1)return false;if(mapTS[c2]==0)mapTS[c2]=c1+1;else if(mapTS[c2]!=c1+1)return false;}return true;}public static void main(String[] args)throws Exception{BufferedReader br=new BufferedReader(new InputStreamReader(System.in));int T=Integer.parseInt(br.readLine().trim());StringBuilder sb=new StringBuilder();for(int tc=0;tc<T;tc++){String s=br.readLine();String t=br.readLine();if(isIsomorphic(s,t))sb.append("YES\n");else sb.append("NO\n");}System.out.print(sb.toString());}}
